beginner.question: What is a 'modern agency website'?
beginner.answer: A portfolio site for a creative/digital agency showcasing services, case studies, team, and client work. It emphasizes visual design, animations, and storytelling. Sections: Hero, Services, Work/Portfolio, Team, Testimonials, Contact.

beginner.question: What is a 'services grid' on an agency site?
beginner.answer: A grid showcasing the agency's offerings (Web Design, Branding, Development, Marketing). Each service card has an icon, title, and short description. Use CSS Grid: `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(280px, 1fr))`.

beginner.question: What is a 'team section' on an agency website?
beginner.answer: A grid of team member cards. Each card has a photo, name, role, and social links. Desktop: 3-4 columns. Tablet: 2 columns. Mobile: 1 column. Use `object-fit: cover` for consistent headshot sizes.

beginner.question: What is the 'hero section' for a creative agency?
beginner.answer: A visually striking hero with a large headline, animated background (video, particles, or bold typography), and a clear CTA. It should immediately communicate the agency's unique value and creative identity.

beginner.question: How do you design a responsive 'case study' card?
beginner.answer: A card with a large project image, client name, project type, and 'View Project' link. Desktop: 2-column grid or overlapping image/text layouts. Mobile: stacked with image on top. Use `aspect-ratio` for consistent image frames.

beginner.question: What is a 'testimonial carousel' on an agency site?
beginner.answer: A rotating set of client testimonials. Desktop: single testimonial with large quote styling or side-by-side. Mobile: single card with swipe/dot navigation. Use CSS scroll-snap or JavaScript for the carousel logic.

beginner.question: What is the typical color scheme for a modern agency website?
beginner.answer: Bold, brand-driven colors: often a dark background (charcoal, deep navy) with bright accent colors (vibrant purple, cyan, gradient). Generous whitespace, large typography, and high-contrast CTAs.

beginner.question: How do you design a responsive 'contact' section?
beginner.answer: Desktop: two columns (contact info + form, or map + form). Mobile: single column, form fields full-width. Include email, phone, address, and social links. The CTA is a prominent 'Send Message' button.

beginner.question: What is a 'client logo bar'?
beginner.answer: A horizontal row of client logos (trust signals). Use grayscale with color-on-hover effect. Flexbox with `flex-wrap: wrap; justify-content: center; gap: 2rem;`. Responsive: fewer visible logos on mobile with scroll.

beginner.question: How do you create a responsive 'navigation' for an agency site?
beginner.answer: Desktop: horizontal nav with logo, links, and CTA button. Mobile: hamburger menu with animated open/close (menu transforms into an X). Consider full-screen overlay menus for a bold agency look.

intermediate.question: How do you create a 'masonry portfolio' grid for agency work?
intermediate.answer: Use CSS Grid with `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(300px, 1fr));` and `grid-row: span N` for taller items. Or use `grid-template-rows: masonry` (Firefox) or a JavaScript masonry library for full browser support.

intermediate.question: How do you implement a 'full-screen overlay navigation'?
intermediate.answer: A fixed overlay: `position: fixed; inset: 0; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.95); z-index: 999; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;`. Animate: `opacity: 0; pointer-events: none;` toggled to `opacity: 1; pointer-events: all;`.

intermediate.question: How do you create a 'split-screen hero' for an agency?
intermediate.answer: 50/50 split: left side has text and CTA, right side has an image, video, or bold graphic. Use `display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;`. On mobile: single column, image on top or bottom with `order`.

intermediate.question: How do you implement a 'hover reveal' effect on case study images?
intermediate.answer: On hover, reveal additional info (project title, client) over the image. Use `position: relative;` on the container. Overlay: `position: absolute; inset: 0; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 0.4s;`. On hover: `opacity: 1;` with background gradient.

intermediate.question: How do you design responsive 'process steps' (how we work)?
intermediate.answer: A horizontal row of connected steps (1. Discover → 2. Design → 3. Develop → 4. Launch). Desktop: horizontal line connecting numbered circles. Mobile: vertical timeline with steps going down. Use `::before` for connecting lines.

intermediate.question: How do you implement a 'parallax scrolling' effect for agency backgrounds?
intermediate.answer: Use `background-attachment: fixed;` for simplicity (works on desktop). For better mobile support, use CSS transforms: `transform: translateY(calc(var(--scroll-offset) * 0.5))`. Respect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`.

intermediate.question: How do you create a 'stats/counter' section with animated numbers?
intermediate.answer: A row of stat cards: 120+ Projects, 8 Years, 25+ Clients, 15 Awards. Desktop: 4 columns. Mobile: 2 columns. Use IntersectionObserver to trigger counting animation. Large bold numbers with descriptive labels below.

intermediate.question: How do you design a responsive 'blog/article preview' section for an agency?
intermediate.answer: A grid of article cards: featured image, category tag, title, excerpt, author, and date. Desktop: 3-column grid. Tablet: 2 columns. Mobile: 1 column. Category tags use color coding.

intermediate.question: How do you implement a 'smooth scroll' navigation for a one-page agency site?
intermediate.answer: Set `scroll-behavior: smooth;` on `<html>`. Use `<a href="#services">` links. Add `scroll-margin-top: 80px;` on sections to account for fixed header offset. For nav-link active state, use IntersectionObserver to highlight current section.

intermediate.question: How do you create a 'gradient border' effect on agency cards?
intermediate.answer: Use `border` with a gradient: `border: 2px solid transparent; background-clip: padding-box;` and a `::before` pseudo-element with `background: linear-gradient(...)` as the actual border. Or use `background-image: linear-gradient(...)` on the wrapper.

advanced.question: How do you implement 'webgl/three.js' backgrounds that degrade gracefully on mobile?
advanced.answer: Load Three.js conditionally: `if (window.innerWidth > 768 && !matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches) { /* init Three.js */ }`. Fallback: CSS gradient or static image background. Provide a pause button.

advanced.question: How do you create a 'magnetic button' hover effect with CSS?
advanced.answer: A magnetic button follows the cursor within its bounds. Pure CSS can approximate with `transform: scale(1.05)` and `transition`. Full magnetic effect requires JavaScript tracking mouse position relative to button center.

advanced.question: How do you implement a 'horizontal scrolling' portfolio section?
advanced.answer: Use `overflow-x: hidden;` on the body while the section is active. Use JavaScript to translate the section horizontally based on scroll position: `transform: translateX(calc(-100vw * (scrollProgress - sectionOffset)))`. Requires careful calculation.

advanced.question: How do you create an 'awards/certifications' badge grid?
advanced.answer: A grid of award badges and logos with hover tooltips. Desktop: 3-4 columns. Mobile: 2 columns. Each badge uses `aspect-ratio: 1;` for consistency. Use CSS `filter: grayscale(1);` with color reveal on hover.

advanced.question: How do you implement 'lazy loading' with progressive image resolution?
advanced.answer: Load a tiny blurred thumbnail (30px wide, base64-encoded) as placeholder. Use `filter: blur(20px);` on the placeholder. On full image load, transition to sharp image. The `` tag transitions from blurry to clear.

advanced.question: How do you create a 'page transition' animation between agency portfolio pages?
advanced.answer: Use `<link rel="prerender">` for the next page. Use CSS `view-transition-name` for shared element transitions. On link click, animate current page out (opacity/transform), change content, animate new page in.

advanced.question: How do you implement a custom 'cursor reveal' effect on hover?
advanced.answer: Replace the default cursor with a custom div: `cursor: none;` on body. On hover over interactive elements, change the custom cursor's size, color, or text. Use `transform: scale()` for size changes. Respect `(hover: hover)` media query.

advanced.question: How do you design a responsive 'careers/jobs' section?
advanced.answer: List open positions with title, department, location, and 'Apply' link. Desktop: table-like layout or list with columns. Mobile: stacked cards. Use `display: grid; grid-template-columns: 2fr 1fr 1fr 100px;` desktop, `1fr` mobile.

advanced.question: How do you implement 'infinite horizontal scroll' for client/partner logos?
advanced.answer: Duplicate the logo list. Use `@keyframes scroll { 0% { transform: translateX(0); } 100% { transform: translateX(-50%); } }`. Apply to the container: `animation: scroll 20s linear infinite;`. Pause on hover: `animation-play-state: paused;`.

advanced.question: How do you create a responsive 'interactive timeline' for agency history?
advanced.answer: Desktop: horizontal timeline with alternating top/bottom entries connected by a line. Mobile: vertical timeline with all entries on one side. Use `::before` for the line and `::after` for dots. Use CSS `scroll-snap` for snap-to-entry navigation.
